output.content:
1|/**
     2| * IVX Deployment Claim Gate.
     3| *
     4| * Root cause fixed by this module (owner-reported incident, 2026-07-10): the
     5| * IVX Owner AI chat returned fabricated deployment confirmations —
     6| * "**Deployment Proceeding**", "### Deployment Confirmation",
     7| * "Deployment ID: [AUTO-GENERATED]", "Commit SHA: [CURRENT SHA]",
     8| * "The changes are live", "Health Check: Passed successfully" — with NO real
     9| * execution behind them. Those replies came from the general chat-model path,
    10| * which was not covered by the developer-execution guard (that guard only runs
    11| * on routed development commands) and the senior-developer narrative gate was
    12| * only applied on the public-chat path.
    13| *
    14| * This gate is applied at the single choke point every visible Owner AI answer
    15| * passes through (`assertVisibleOwnerAIAnswer`), so no path can bypass it.
    16| *
    17| * Hard rules:
    18| *  - Placeholder proof values ([AUTO-GENERATED], [CURRENT SHA], [placeholder],
    19| *    bracketed deployment IDs / commit SHAs) are NEVER valid — they are blocked
    20| *    unconditionally, even when other text looks like evidence.
    21| *  - Deployment-success narratives ("Deployment Proceeding", "Deployment
    22| *    Confirmation", "The changes are live", "Health check passed",
    23| *    "successfully deployed") are blocked unless the answer carries REAL
    24| *    evidence: raw command output (`$ cmd ... exit code: N`) or a real commit
    25| *    hash / live health proof line.
    26| *  - The replacement answer states the honest deployment state
    27| *    (NOT_DEPLOYED) from the deployment state machine — never a success claim.
    28| *
    29| * Pure + deterministic (no I/O, network, or AI) so it is fully unit-testable.
    30| */
    31|
    32|import { hasDeploymentProof, hasRawCommandOutput } from './ivx-developer-execution-guard';
    33|
    34|export const IVX_DEPLOYMENT_CLAIM_GATE_MARKER = 'ivx-deployment-claim-gate-2026-07-10';
    35|
    36|/**
    37| * Placeholder proof values. A real deployment report NEVER contains these —
    38| * they are template slots the model failed to fill with real tool output.
    39| * Blocked unconditionally.
    40| */
    41|const PLACEHOLDER_DEPLOYMENT_VALUES: readonly { marker: RegExp; label: string }[] = [
    42|  { marker: /\[auto[-\s_]?generated\]/i, label: 'placeholder deployment ID (AUTO-GENERATED)' },
    43|  { marker: /\[current[-\s_]?sha\]/i, label: 'placeholder commit SHA (CURRENT SHA)' },
    44|  { marker: /\[placeholder\]/i, label: 'literal placeholder token' },
    45|  { marker: /\[pending\]/i, label: 'pending placeholder token' },
    46|  { marker: /\[unknown\]/i, label: 'unknown placeholder token' },
    47|  { marker: /\[example[^\]]*\]/i, label: 'example placeholder token' },
    48|  { marker: /\b(?:deployment|deploy)\s*id\s*:?\**\s*\[[^\]]*\]/i, label: 'bracketed deployment ID slot' },
    49|  { marker: /\bcommit(?:\s*sha)?\s*:?\**\s*\[[^\]]*\]/i, label: 'bracketed commit SHA slot' },
    50|];
    51|
    52|/**
    53| * Fabricated deployment-confirmation narratives. Blocked when the answer lacks
    54| * real execution evidence. Sourced from the exact bad responses the owner
    55| * reported from the live chat.
    56| */
    57|const FAKE_DEPLOYMENT_CONFIRMATIONS: readonly { marker: RegExp; label: string }[] = [
    58|  { marker: /deployment\s+proceeding/i, label: 'Deployment Proceeding template' },
    59|  { marker: /deployment\s+confirmation/i, label: 'Deployment Confirmation template' },
    60|  { marker: /the\s+changes\s+are\s+(?:now\s+)?live/i, label: 'fabricated changes-are-live claim' },
    61|  { marker: /changes\s+have\s+been\s+successfully\s+deployed/i, label: 'fabricated deployed-successfully claim' },
    62|  { marker: /successfully\s+deployed\s+to\s+(?:production|prod|render|live)/i, label: 'fabricated deployed-to-production claim' },
    63|  { marker: /health\s+check(?:s)?\s*:?\**\s*(?:passed|✅)/i, label: 'Health check passed claim' },
    64|  { marker: /health\s+check(?:s)?\s+passed\s+successfully/i, label: 'Health check passed successfully claim' },
    65|  { marker: /running\s+post-?deployment\s+health\s+checks/i, label: 'post-deployment health checks narrative' },
    66|  { marker: /deploying\s+(?:changes\s+)?(?:now\s+)?(?:to\s+production|based\s+on\s+your\s+approval)/i, label: 'deploying-now narrative' },
    67|];
    68|
    69|/** Placeholder proof values present in an answer. Pure — deterministic. */
    70|export function findPlaceholderDeploymentValues(answer: string): string[] {
    71|  const text = typeof answer === 'string' ? answer : '';
    72|  return PLACEHOLDER_DEPLOYMENT_VALUES.filter(({ marker }) => marker.test(text)).map(({ label }) => label);
    73|}
    74|
    75|/** Fabricated deployment-confirmation markers present in an answer. Pure. */
    76|export function findFakeDeploymentConfirmations(answer: string): string[] {
    77|  const text = typeof answer === 'string' ? answer : '';
    78|  return FAKE_DEPLOYMENT_CONFIRMATIONS.filter(({ marker }) => marker.test(text)).map(({ label }) => label);
    79|}
    80|
    81|/**
    82| * Real deployment evidence: raw command output (`$ cmd ... exit code: N`) or a
    83| * real commit hash / live health proof line. Only answers produced by the real
    84| * execution pipeline contain these.
    85| */
    86|export function hasRealDeploymentEvidence(answer: string): boolean {
    87|  const text = typeof answer === 'string' ? answer : '';
    88|  return hasRawCommandOutput(text) || hasDeploymentProof(text);
    89|}
    90|
    91|/**
    92| * The honest replacement returned when a fake deployment narrative is blocked.
    93| * States the true deployment-state-machine position (NOT_DEPLOYED) and the only
    94| * valid path to a COMPLETE status. Never contains a success claim.
    95| */
    96|export function buildRejectedDeploymentNarrativeAnswer(violations: string[]): string {
    97|  const reasons = violations.length > 0 ? violations.map((v) => ` - ${v}`).join('\n') : ' - fabricated deployment narrative';
    98|  return [
    99|    `GATE=${IVX_DEPLOYMENT_CLAIM_GATE_MARKER}`,
   100|    'DEPLOYMENT_STATE=NOT_DEPLOYED',
   101|    'FINAL_STATUS=BLOCKED_FAKE_DEPLOYMENT_NARRATIVE',
   102|    `REASON=The generated reply claimed deployment success without real execution evidence. Rejected markers:\n${reasons}`,
   103|    'REQUIRED_EVIDENCE=real GitHub commit SHA, real branch, real push result, real Render deployment ID and status, production URL, /health HTTP status, live commit from /version, and an exact GitHub-to-production commit match.',
   104|    'EXACT_ACTION_REQUIRED=Run the real Senior Developer pipeline (Admin → IVX Developer Workspace → Patches → Run Senior Developer). Deployment may only be reported from its recorded evidence — never from generated text.',
   105|  ].join('\n');
   106|}
   107|
   108|export type DeploymentClaimGateResult = {
   109|  /** The answer to return (rewritten when the gate intervenes). */
   110|  answer: string;
   111|  /** Whether the gate replaced the answer. */
   112|  gated: boolean;
   113|  /** The violations that triggered the gate. */
   114|  violations: string[];
   115|};
   116|
   117|/**
   118| * Apply the Deployment Claim Gate to a visible chat answer.
   119| *
   120| *  - Placeholder proof values block unconditionally.
   121| *  - Fake deployment confirmations block unless real evidence is present.
   122| *  - Idempotent: a previously gated answer (carrying the gate marker) passes.
   123| *
   124| * Pure — deterministic, no I/O.
   125| */
   126|export function applyDeploymentClaimGate(input: { answer: string }): DeploymentClaimGateResult {
   127|  const text = typeof input.answer === 'string' ? input.answer : '';
   128|
   129|  // Idempotency sentinel — the honest blocked answer quotes violation labels
   130|  // and must never re-trigger the gate on itself.
   131|  if (text.includes(IVX_DEPLOYMENT_CLAIM_GATE_MARKER)) {
   132|    return { answer: text, gated: false, violations: [] };
   133|  }
   134|
   135|  const placeholderViolations = findPlaceholderDeploymentValues(text);
   136|  const confirmationViolations = findFakeDeploymentConfirmations(text);
   137|
   138|  // Placeholders are never valid, evidence or not.
   139|  if (placeholderViolations.length > 0) {
   140|    const violations = [...placeholderViolations, ...confirmationViolations];
   141|    return { answer: buildRejectedDeploymentNarrativeAnswer(violations), gated: true, violations };
   142|  }
   143|
   144|  // Deployment-success narratives require real evidence.
   145|  if (confirmationViolations.length > 0 && !hasRealDeploymentEvidence(text)) {
   146|    return {
   147|      answer: buildRejectedDeploymentNarrativeAnswer(confirmationViolations),
   148|      gated: true,
   149|      violations: confirmationViolations,
   150|    };
   151|  }
   152|
   153|  return { answer: text, gated: false, violations: [] };
   154|}

(End of file - total 154 lines)

responseMessageContents.text:
I need to update the deployment claim gate by adding the new placeholder tokens to the values list and extending the prohibited phrases to catch more deployment confirmation language.

Then I'll create a pure service module for the 12-state deployment state machine with the 18-field evidence validator, wire it into the deployment report generation, and update the corresponding tests. I should be careful about false positives—phrases like "Fix complete" are broad, but the user explicitly wants them blocked, and since the gate only blocks when there's no real evidence backing the claim, it should be fine. I also need to ensure the gate labels themselves don't trigger the gate through the idempotency sentinel.

Let me check the existing test file and narrative gate to keep the implementation consistent.

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm extending the deployment claim gate service with additional confirmation phrases like "Deployment successful," "Build completed," "Changes applied," and "Live on Render," along with new placeholder tokens such as MOCK, NARRATIVE, GENERATED, SIMULATED, and ESTIMATED for the evidence fields.

Next I'm creating a state machine module that enforces a strict 12-state progression from repo frozen through production verified, requiring 18 specific evidence fields and rejecting any missing or invalid data. The module will generate deployment reports that return either VERIFIED or UNVERIFIED status with reasoning, and validate health checks by confirming HTTP 200 responses match the running commit SHA.

After writing tests for both the new state machine and extended gate, I need to find where deployment reports are generated in the enterprise deployment engine and wire the validation there, keeping the changes minimal by focusing on the ivx-owner-ai answer path that's already covered by the claim gate.

I'm being careful about false positives in the phrase gate — "Fix complete" and "Build completed" shouldn't block without real evidence, which is already how confirmations work. The narrative gate's `(?<!UN)VERIFIED` marker is fine as-is. Now I'm looking at how the enterprise deployment engine collects evidence so I can integrate the validator there.

I'll also need to write tests covering both the extended claim gate and the new state machine functionality.

Now I need to figure out where to integrate the state machine validation into the enterprise deployment engine's report path — specifically, I'm looking for where the engine computes the final status and assembles evidence so I can hook the validation in at the right point.

Now I'm building a new state machine module that defines the twelve ordered deployment states, validates transitions to prevent skipping ahead, and creates a comprehensive evidence report type with eighteen fields to track deployment verification across the entire lifecycle.

responseMessageContents.text:
Now I'm moving into implementation: extending the deployment claim gate with additional prohibited phrases and forbidden evidence values, then building out a comprehensive state machine with twelve states and eighteen evidence fields that validates health and constructs evidence-only reports. I'll need tests for both components and then wire everything together.

The key decision is how to handle the engine's `COMPLETE` status — it's already evidence-derived from real API calls, but I should add a strict validation check before returning it. I'll make `COMPLETE` require commitMatch, healthy health status, and a present renderDeployId; otherwise downgrade to `UNVERIFIED`. I should also sync the new gate markers into the narrative gate for consistency across both paths, then run the backend test suite to verify everything.

edits.newString:
const FAKE_DEPLOYMENT_CONFIRMATIONS: readonly { marker: RegExp; label: string }[] = [
  { marker: /deployment\s+proceeding/i, label: 'Deployment Proceeding template' },
  { marker: /deployment\s+confirmation/i, label: 'Deployment Confirmation template' },
  { marker: /the\s+changes\s+are\s+(?:now\s+)?live/i, label: 'fabricated changes-are-live claim' },
  { marker: /changes\s+have\s+been\s+successfully\s+deployed/i, label: 'fabricated deployed-successfully claim' },
  { marker: /successfully\s+deployed\s+to\s+(?:production|prod|render|live)/i, label: 'fabricated deployed-to-production claim' },
  { marker: /health\s+check(?:s)?\s*:?\**\s*(?:passed|✅)/i, label: 'Health check passed claim' },
  { marker: /health\s+check(?:s)?\s+passed\s+successfully/i, label: 'Health check passed successfully claim' },
  { marker: /running\s+post-?deployment\s+health\s+checks/i, label: 'post-deployment health checks narrative' },
  { marker: /deploying\s+(?:changes\s+)?(?:now\s+)?(?:to\s+production|based\s+on\s+your\s+approval)/i, label: 'deploying-now narrative' },
  // Owner spec (2026-07-11) Step 0: prohibited narrative phrases. Blocked
  // unless the answer carries REAL execution evidence.
  { marker: /deployment\s+(?:was\s+|is\s+|has\s+been\s+)?successful/i, label: 'fabricated Deployment successful claim' },
  { marker: /\bsuccessfully\s+deployed\b/i, label: 'fabricated Successfully deployed claim' },
  { marker: /build\s+(?:was\s+|is\s+|has\s+been\s+)?(?:completed|successful)/i, label: 'fabricated Build completed claim' },
  { marker: /changes\s+(?:were\s+|have\s+been\s+|are\s+)?applied/i, label: 'fabricated Changes applied claim' },
  { marker: /production\s+(?:was\s+|is\s+|has\s+been\s+)?updated/i, label: 'fabricated Production updated claim' },
  { marker: /\blive\s+on\s+render\b/i, label: 'fabricated Live on Render claim' },
  { marker: /\bfix\s+(?:is\s+)?complete\b/i, label: 'fabricated Fix complete claim' },
];

{"filePath": "backend/services/ivx-deployment-claim-gate.ts", "edits": [{"oldString": "const PLACEHOLDER_DEPLOYMENT_VALUES: readonly { marker: RegExp; label: string }[] = [
  { marker: /\[auto[-\s_]?generated\]/i, label: 'placeholder deployment ID (AUTO-GENERATED)' },

responseMessageContents.text: The work is done and tests are passing, so I'm ready to generate the final Step 9 report with the evidence. The gate fix is complete and verified locally, though it hasn't been deployed yet due to a GitHub token issue, so I'll mark it as unverified in the report.

# STEP 1–9 COMPLETE IN CODE — ALL LOCALLY VERIFIED. PRODUCTION REMAINS UNVERIFIED (push still blocked)

## Step 1 — Root cause (from repo audit)

Fake narratives do **not** come from hardcoded templates, mocks, or static JSON — repo-wide search found zero hardcoded success strings in response paths. They are **AI-generated template text** from the general chat-model path, which previously bypassed validation. The choke-point gate (`assertVisibleOwnerAIAnswer`, all 26 answer paths) already exists from the prior fix; this task extended it to your full spec.

## Steps 2–4 — Gate extended (`backend/services/ivx-deployment-claim-gate.ts`)

- **All 8 prohibited phrases now blocked** without real evidence: "Deployment successful", "Build completed", "Changes applied", "Production updated", "Health check passed", "Live on Render", "Successfully deployed", "Fix complete" — each covered by a regression test
- **All 10 forbidden evidence values block unconditionally** (even alongside real command output): AUTO-GENERATED, UNKNOWN, PENDING, PLACEHOLDER, MOCK, NARRATIVE, GENERATED, SIMULATED, ESTIMATED, ASSUMED — e.g. `Deployment ID: SIMULATED` → answer rejected, replaced with `DEPLOYMENT_STATE=NOT_DEPLOYED / FINAL_STATUS=BLOCKED_FAKE_DEPLOYMENT_NARRATIVE`
- Same markers added to the public-chat narrative gate (`ivx-senior-developer-narrative-gate.ts`) for path parity

## Steps 5–9 — NEW `backend/services/ivx-deployment-state-machine.ts`

- Exactly your 12 states `REPO_FROZEN → … → PRODUCTION_VERIFIED`; `advanceDeploymentState` rejects any skip or backward transition (test: `CODE_CHANGED → PRODUCTION_VERIFIED` = rejected)
- All 18 evidence fields typed and required; any missing field → `UNVERIFIED` with `missing evidence field: <name>`
- Step 7 enforced: HTTP must be exactly 200, `runningCommitSha` must prefix-match `commitSha`, SHAs must be real hex (7–40 chars) — "CURRENT SHA" / bracketed values rejected
- Step 8 enforced: `qaResult` FAIL **or not run** → `UNVERIFIED`
- Step 9 report renderer prints only evidence; empty slots print `UNVERIFIED`, never invented values

## Evidence of execution (real command output)

- Targeted gate suites: `bun test` → **96 pass, 0 fail (269 assertions)**
- Full backend suite: `bun test ./backend` → **870 pass, 0 fail (3,258 assertions, 75 files)**
- Typecheck on all 3 changed/new modules: `tsc --noEmit --strict` → **exit 0**

## Step 9 — Final report for THIS task's deployment state

```text
Repository: ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest
Branch: main
Commit SHA: UNVERIFIED
Deployment Platform: render
Deployment ID: UNVERIFIED
Deployment Status: UNVERIFIED
Deployment Timestamp: UNVERIFIED
Production URL: https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com
Health URL: https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com/health
HTTP Status: 200 (serving OLD commit)
Running Commit SHA: d35db8b9 (does NOT include this fix)
Runtime Version: ivx-owner-ai-backend
QA Result: UNVERIFIED (cannot run — fix not in production)

FINAL STATUS: UNVERIFIED
REASON = Missing production evidence — GitHub push blocked: stored token ghp_****QTOt returns 401 Bad credentials; no commit, no deploy, no production verification possible.
```

The one remaining blocker is unchanged: provide a valid `GITHUB_TOKEN` (repo scope) and I will immediately push, trigger the Render deploy, and return this same report with every field filled from real API responses.

responseMessageContents.title: Improved the reliability of project status reports to ensure they reflect real progress.
responseMessageContents.description: Updated the system to prevent incorrect or placeholder status messages during app updates. The application now requires real evidence from internal checks and server tests before confirming any task is complete. This ensures that all information provided about the app's health and deployment is accurate and verified.
